title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, is set to begin her trial on March 23 for assault with a dangerous weapon.
Fitzsimmons, shot at home by a colleague, survived the incident while being served a restraining order last summer.
The bench trial, overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp at Essex Superior Court, involves conflicting accounts and mental health issues.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Sinisterra Mazariegos uses marathons to process emotions and understand personal growth.
She dedicates her run to patients rebuilding their lives and the Spaulding professionals supporting them.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with opening act The Chats on Friday.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist led energetic performances, engaging the audience with theatrical showmanship.
The concert included a mix of new songs and classics, showcasing the band's enduring appeal and tight performance.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame the challenge through treatment and perseverance.
McHugh runs in memory of her late mentor Susan Hurley and for support to cancer-related charities.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others urged Meta to address facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy risks.
Lawmakers demand Meta clarify data handling and opt-out options for facial recognition by April 6.
Concerns revolve around potential misuse at protests, with technology possibly tracking individuals unknowingly.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy's City Council delayed voting on a $2.65 million plan to replace firefighting gear containing PFAS.
Mayor Thomas Koch proposed PFAS-free gear after testing revealed harmful chemicals in current equipment.
Firefighters expressed frustration over the delay, citing health risks linked to PFAS exposure.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce costs and focus on native wildlife.
The zoo requires $57 million in upgrades and incurs $900,000 annually from the general fund to operate.
Operations will remain unchanged until September 7, aiming for free weekend access and outdoor space redevelopment thereafter.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a last-minute microwedding at Bow Market in Somerville due to a blizzard.
Kara Baskin reflects on 1990s communication styles in the FX series about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette.
Meredith Goldstein questions the frequent use of 'partner' in relationships, considering its implications on dating dynamics.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback from Burlington is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH, honoring his late father and surviving cancer.
Averback's father was treated for glioblastoma at Massachusetts General Hospital, and he was later diagnosed with testicular cancer.
After recovering from a serious blood infection, Averback resumed training, viewing running as therapy and a tribute to resilience.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots have shown interest in acquiring Eagles WR A.J. Brown, but Philadelphia declined their recent trade offer.
Brown is working out with new Patriots safety Kevin Byard; they previously played together with the Titans and Eagles.
The Eagles may trade Brown after June 1, seeking a first-round draft pick, while Byard and Brown's interactions fuel speculation.
